tools_called.name: system_architecture_designer

args.system_design: A comprehensive city planning scheme for Datong, Shanxi Province, China. The city is a historic coal-mining and cultural hub (~3.1 million population, ~1,000 km² urban area) located in a basin at ~1,050 m average elevation. The plan must integrate: (1) Architectural design blending traditional Shanxi courtyard (siheyuan) and Yungang Grottoes heritage with modern sustainable buildings; (2) Multi-modal transportation network including high-speed rail (existing Datong-Zhangjiakou HSR), BRT, metro/light rail, EV infrastructure, and pedestrian-friendly streets; (3) Environmental protection addressing air quality (coal transition), green belts, renewable energy (solar/wind in the Datong Basin), water management for the Sanggan River watershed, and ecological restoration of mining areas.
args.technology_stack: Smart city IoT sensors, GIS-based urban modeling (ArcGIS/QGIS), BIM for architectural design, Digital Twin platform, AI-driven traffic management, renewable energy microgrids, green building materials (low-carbon concrete, cross-laminated timber), water recycling systems, air quality monitoring networks
args.hardware_requirements: Distributed air quality sensors (PM2.5/PM10/NOx/SO2), smart traffic cameras and inductive loop detectors, solar PV arrays (target 500 MW), wind turbines along mountain corridors, EV charging stations (1 per 500m in urban core), smart water meters, geothermal heat pump systems for district heating, 5G small cells for IoT backbone, flood monitoring stations along Sanggan River
args.interaction_interface: Unified City Dashboard (web + mobile) for residents and administrators; real-time air quality map; smart public transit app with multi-modal routing; citizen reporting portal for infrastructure issues; VR/AR heritage tourism interface for Yungang Grottoes and Huayan Temple; digital twin visualization for planners
args.test_optimization_plan: Phased rollout: Phase 1 (Years 1-3) - Air quality sensor network + BRT corridors + solar pilot; Phase 2 (Years 4-7) - Metro Line 1 + green belt expansion + mining restoration; Phase 3 (Years 8-12) - Full digital twin + renewable grid integration + heritage district revitalization. KPIs: PM2.5 reduction to WHO guidelines, 40% green space coverage, 60% public transit mode share, 30% renewable energy mix, zero coal for residential heating by Year 10.
